problem: Eric builds a small pyramid for a school project. His pyramid has a height of twelve inches and a square base that measures ten inches on each side. Eric wants to find the smallest cube-shaped box to put his pyramid in so that he can safely bring it to school right side up. What is the volume of this box, in inches cubed?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the dimensions of Eric's pyramid for his school project?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the height of the pyramid and the length of each side of the square base?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Why does Eric want to find a cube-shaped box for the pyramid?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What are the minimum dimensions for the length, width, and height of the box based on the dimensions of the pyramid?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why the height of the pyramid determines the minimum height of the box?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: If we chose a box that measures the same length and width as the base of the pyramid, could it accommodate the height of the pyramid? Why or why not?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the volume of the cube-shaped box that can accommodate the pyramid?
classified_questions.label: b
